name: che-livedocs-mcp
description: Primary-source-first, always-latest documentation engine — auto-discovers llms.txt / package registry / repo / OpenAPI / GraphQL / CLI, reads them live and raw. A high-fidelity alternative to pre-built docs indexes.
long_description: LiveDocs fetches the LATEST documentation for any library from its canonical machine-readable PRIMARY source, on demand. Where a pre-built vector index is periodically recrawled and lossy, LiveDocs auto-discovers each library's source live (llms.txt → npm/PyPI registry → GitHub repo → OpenAPI/GraphQL/CLI introspection) and can return it RAW. It resolves the exact latest version from the registry and reads the API contract itself via introspection — dimensions a pre-built index structurally cannot reach. Results are ranked fidelity-first, then freshness; when no primary source exists it says so, so you can fall back to a generic index and label it low-fidelity. 4 tools: resolve_source, fetch_docs, latest_version, introspect.

tools.name: resolve_source
tools.description: Discover ranked primary documentation sources for a library (llms.txt → registry → repo), freshest+highest-fidelity first

tools.name: fetch_docs
tools.description: Fetch the raw verbatim text of a source URL — the high-fidelity path, no lossy index between

tools.name: latest_version
tools.description: Deterministic latest released version + changelog/repo URLs from the package registry

tools.name: introspect
tools.description: Read the machine contract itself: OpenAPI/Swagger spec, GraphQL schema, or an installed CLI's --help/--version
